questions.question: What is the primary objective of an AI-based Quality Inspection System in Industry 4.0?

answers.b: To automatically inspect products and identify manufacturing defects

explanations.a: Incorrect. Machine temperature is monitored for maintenance purposes and is unrelated to quality inspection.
explanations.b: Correct. AI-based quality inspection automatically detects manufacturing defects using computer vision and machine learning, improving inspection speed and consistency.
explanations.c: Incorrect. AI inspection systems complement robotic automation but do not replace industrial robots.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Quality inspection focuses on product quality rather than energy consumption.

questions.question: Which component captures images of products for AI-based inspection?

explanations.a: Incorrect. A PLC controls industrial equipment but does not capture images.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Compressors generate compressed air and are unrelated to image acquisition.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Electric motors provide mechanical motion and are not imaging devices.
explanations.d: Correct. Industrial cameras acquire high-quality images that are processed by computer vision algorithms for defect detection.

questions.question: Computer Vision in manufacturing is primarily used to:

answers.c: Analyze images and identify product defects

explanations.a: Incorrect. Power generation is unrelated to computer vision.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Lighting supports image acquisition but is not the primary function of computer vision.
explanations.c: Correct. Computer Vision analyzes product images to detect defects, verify dimensions, and automate quality inspection.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Conveyor speed is controlled by automation systems rather than vision algorithms.

explanations.a: Incorrect. This does not represent inspection accuracy.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Inspection speed and product size do not directly calculate inspection accuracy.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Camera focus influences performance but is not the mathematical definition of inspection accuracy.
explanations.d: Correct. Inspection Accuracy measures the percentage of products correctly classified by the AI inspection system.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common manufacturing defect detected by AI vision systems?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Internet bandwidth is a network parameter and not a product defect.
explanations.b: Correct. Surface scratches are common visual defects detected during automated quality inspection.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Battery voltage is an electrical parameter.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Network latency relates to communication systems, not product inspection.

questions.question: What does the AI Confidence Score represent during product inspection?

answers.a: The operating speed of the conveyor belt
answers.b: The total number of inspected products
answers.c: The electrical power consumed by the camera
answers.d: The probability that the AI has correctly identified the detected defect

explanations.a: Incorrect. Conveyor speed influences inspection throughput but is not represented by the confidence score.
explanations.b: Incorrect. The number of inspected products is a production metric, not an AI confidence metric.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Camera power consumption is unrelated to AI prediction confidence.
explanations.d: Correct. The AI Confidence Score indicates how certain the AI model is about its prediction. A higher confidence score generally represents a more reliable defect classification.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of setting a Defect Threshold in an AI quality inspection system?

answers.a: To define the maximum permissible defect size before a product is rejected

explanations.a: Correct. The Defect Threshold determines the acceptable defect limit. Products exceeding this limit are classified as defective and rejected.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Conveyor speed affects production throughput but is unrelated to defect threshold.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Camera resolution is a hardware specification and is not controlled by the defect threshold.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Electrical power calculations are unrelated to defect threshold settings.

questions.question: Why is Precision an important metric in AI-based quality inspection?

answers.b: It measures how many rejected products are actually defective

explanations.a: Incorrect. Conveyor efficiency is unrelated to AI precision.
explanations.b: Correct. Precision evaluates the accuracy of defect predictions by indicating how many products identified as defective are truly defective. High precision reduces unnecessary rejection of good products.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Camera brightness affects image quality but is not measured by precision.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Precision is an AI performance metric, not an energy metric.

questions.question: Increasing Inspection Speed without improving the imaging system may result in:

answers.d: Lower inspection accuracy due to reduced processing time

explanations.a: Incorrect. Increasing inspection speed does not eliminate manufacturing defects.
explanations.b: Incorrect. AI confidence does not automatically increase with inspection speed.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Image resolution depends on camera hardware rather than inspection speed.
explanations.d: Correct. Very high inspection speeds reduce the available image acquisition and processing time, which may decrease detection accuracy.

questions.question: Why is AI-based quality inspection preferred over manual inspection in modern smart factories?

answers.a: It only works without industrial cameras
answers.b: It completely eliminates the need for manufacturing processes
answers.c: It provides faster, more consistent, and more accurate defect detection

explanations.a: Incorrect. Industrial cameras are fundamental components of AI-based visual inspection systems.
explanations.b: Incorrect. AI supports manufacturing but does not replace manufacturing processes themselves.
explanations.c: Correct. AI inspection systems operate continuously with high speed, repeatability, and accuracy, reducing human error and improving product quality.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The purpose of AI inspection is to reduce defects, not increase them.
